title: Memory Persistence
subtitle: Memory Persistence
description: How to store and retrieve user preferences, historical context, and factual knowledge across sessions. The agent's "long-term memory" capability.
sub_problems: Memory storage: selecting appropriate storage backends (vector databases, key-value stores, file systems)
Memory retrieval: efficiently retrieving relevant memories
Memory updates: how new information updates or overwrites old memories
Memory expiration: cleanup strategies for outdated information
Cross-session: sharing memory across different sessions
best_practices: LLM extraction outperforms manual logging: LLM captures implicit preferences and context from conversations, while manual add_fact operations are too coarse-grained
Debouncing is mandatory: 30s window + thread_id deduplication to avoid wasted LLM calls in high-frequency conversation scenarios
Confidence gating: not all information deserves to be remembered, 0.7 threshold filters low-value facts
Configuration over hardcoding: all 8 parameters should be configurable, supporting runtime dynamic adjustment
Atomic writes protect data: temp + rename pattern prevents data corruption from interrupted writes
Middleware pattern keeps business logic transparent: memory read/write operations should not intrude into business code

values.DeerFlow(102.03): MemoryUpdateQueue 30s debouncing + thread_id deduplication + batch processing
values.DeerFlow(1002.05): Gateway REST API runtime dynamic modification

values.DeerFlow(102.03): LLM structured output (5 fact types + 0.7 confidence threshold)
Single JSON file + atomic writes (temp + rename)
MemoryMiddleware after_agent auto-enqueue + format_memory_for_injection() injects <memory> tags

solutions.title: Three-layer memory structure + MemoryUpdateQueue debouncing + LLM auto fact extraction
solutions.description: User/history/facts three-layer memory stored in memory.json: user layer contains workContext (2-3 sentences)/personalContext (1-2 sentences)/topOfMind (3-5 sentences), history layer contains recentMonths (4-6 sentences)/earlierContext (3-5 sentences)/longTermBackground (2-4 sentences), facts layer is a discrete fact list (5 types: preference/knowledge/context/behavior/goal). MemoryUpdateQueue implements 30s debouncing (threading.Timer) + thread_id deduplication + threading.Lock concurrency safety + _processing flag prevents re-entrance + 0.5s interval between batch operations. MemoryUpdater uses LLM to analyze conversations and extract structured JSON (6-dimensional context shouldUpdate judgment + newFacts confidence filtering + factsToRemove cleanup), atomic writes (temp + rename). MemoryMiddleware.after_agent() filters messages (retains only human + AI without tool_calls) before enqueuing.

solutions.title: MemoryConfig Pydantic configuration + Gateway API exposure
solutions.description: MemoryConfig (BaseModel) defines 8 configurable parameters: enabled (bool)/storage_path (str)/debounce_seconds (int, ge=1, le=300)/model_name (Optional[str])/max_facts (int, ge=10, le=500)/fact_confidence_threshold (float, ge=0.0, le=1.0)/injection_enabled (bool)/max_injection_tokens (int, ge=100, le=10000). Global singleton pattern: get_memory_config() thread-safe retrieval, load_memory_config_from_dict() loads from dictionary. Gateway API exposes 3 REST endpoints (GET/PUT /api/memory/config + POST /api/memory/config/reset). Consumed by 6 modules: queue/updater/middleware/prompt/gateway router/lead_agent.

solutions.title: Multi-layer memory chain persistence solution
solutions.description: Educational tutoring agents need to maintain multiple memory types during a single problem-solving process: knowledge fragments collected during investigation, step chains during solving, tool call reference tracing, and cross-session conversation history. These memories have different lifecycles and access patterns—investigation memory has high-frequency read/write during analysis loops, solution chains append sequentially as steps progress, reference memory requires global deduplication and ID mapping, and session memory requires cross-request persistence.
